nav_hints.dashboard: Personal summary, account indicators and recent events.
nav_hints.profile: Basic identity, email, role and tenant data.
nav_hints.recovery: Password, MFA, lost-email and compromised-account recovery options.
nav_hints.activity: Recent security and recovery events for your account.
nav_hints.mfa_manage: Open the MFA status and management surface.
nav_hints.mfa_recovery: Request a secure email link to reset MFA access.
nav_hints.recovery_support: Open the assisted recovery request form.
nav_hints.recovery_compromised: Report a hacked or compromised account.

profile.title: Profile
profile.lead: Shows the base information the framework has for your session and tenant context.
profile.identity_hint: This view defines what belongs to the user without mixing system administration.
profile.name: Name
profile.scope_hint: At this stage the profile is informational; editing must be added with dedicated requests and audit.
profile.editing_note: Profile editing stays separate so identity changes are not mixed with recovery flows.

mfa.title: MFA
mfa.lead: Manage second-factor status and safe recovery for lost access.
mfa.status_hint: MFA changes remain delegated to the existing framework flow.
mfa.enabled_hint: The account requires a second factor to complete access.
mfa.disabled_hint: The account currently has no active second factor.
mfa.scope_note: This surface focuses only on the current MFA state. Recovery requests are separated under the MFA submenu so the page does not become a navigation hub.

activity.title: Recent Activity
activity.lead: Lists recovery and security events related to your account.

recovery_public.start_lead: Choose the scenario that best describes your situation. Cases without email access require assisted review.
recovery_public.forgot_password_hint: I have email access and need to change my password.
recovery_public.lost_mfa_hint: I know my password and have email access.
recovery_public.lost_email_hint: I need support to validate the account.
recovery_public.compromised_hint: I suspect unauthorized access or takeover.
recovery_public.mfa_lead: Enter the account email. If it exists and has MFA enabled, we will send a temporary link. The response is always generic for security.
recovery_public.generic_response_hint: For security, we do not confirm whether the email exists or MFA is enabled.
recovery_public.support_lead: Describe your case. Support must manually review requests when primary email access is unavailable.

request_types.lost_email_password: No email access and forgot password
request_types.lost_email_mfa: No email access and lost MFA

messages.mfa_recovery_sent: If the account exists and applies, a recovery link will be sent.
messages.mfa_recovery_success: MFA was reset. Sign in and configure MFA again if the system requires it.
messages.recovery_token_invalid: The recovery link is invalid or expired.
messages.support_request_created: Request received. Support must review the case before approving any change.

events.generic_body: Event registered by the account center.
events.dashboard_view_body: No recovery events are registered for this account yet.

email.mfa_reset_greeting: Hello :name, we received a request to reset MFA for your account.
email.mfa_reset_expiry: The link expires in 30 minutes and can be used only once.
email.mfa_reset_ignore: If you did not request this change, ignore this message and contact support.

module.admin_recovery_hint: Review assisted recovery and compromised-account requests.
module.manage_recovery_description: Allows reviewing and marking assisted account recovery requests.

index.title: Account recovery requests
index.description: Initial queue to review lost-email, lost-MFA or compromised-account cases.

show.title: Recovery request
show.description: Assisted review. Approving or rejecting does not automatically change password, email or MFA without a later dedicated flow.
show.review_hint: Mark the case according to the initial identity and risk review.
show.already_reviewed: This request has already been reviewed.

messages.review_failed: The review could not be saved.

mfa_recovery.title: Request MFA recovery
mfa_recovery.lead: Use this option when the account owner still has access to the registered email but lost the authenticator app or backup codes.
mfa_recovery.form_hint: The link is sent to the current account email and can be used only once.
mfa_recovery.note: For security, the recovery token is stored hashed, expires quickly and resets MFA only when consumed successfully.
mfa_recovery.warning: If the email account is also unavailable, use assisted support recovery instead of this direct flow.

support.title: Assisted support recovery
support.lead: Create a support-reviewed case when direct email recovery is not enough or unauthorized access is suspected.
support.note: Support review does not automatically change password, email or MFA. It records the case and keeps the decision auditable.
support.security_note: Do not share passwords or MFA codes. Describe the issue and use an alternate email where support can contact you.
